subheading: Increase image resolution and sharpen detail with open, editable ComfyUI workflows that let you pick the model and keep skin and texture natural.
extendedDescription:
The honest worry about any ai image upscaler is the plastic face. You enlarge a portrait, and the skin comes back waxy: pores gone, eyes glassy, fine wrinkles smoothed into a mask. That happens because most upscalers chase a clean result and replace lost micro-texture with blur. The ComfyUI workflows on this page take a different stance. You choose a texture-preserving model and set the denoise and detail strength yourself, so you decide how much the model invents and how much of the real surface it keeps.

It helps to know what AI upscaling actually does. A plain resize stretches the pixels you already have, so a small photo just gets bigger and softer. AI upscaling reconstructs detail instead, predicting the edges and surfaces that a higher-resolution version would contain. That reconstruction is what makes faces look sharp again, and it is also why the wrong model smooths skin into plastic. The fix is control, not a magic setting, and these workflows expose it.

The cluster covers real models for different jobs. SeedVR2 is the general-purpose choice when you want to upscale image content cleanly while keeping texture. Z-Image-Turbo runs a fast 2K pass when you need a quick enlarge without a long wait. Magnific comes in two flavors: a creative mode that adds invented detail for a richer look, and a precise mode that stays faithful to the source when accuracy matters more than flair. Topaz enhances and sharpens existing photos, and Stability offers a fast upscale for everyday work. For motion, SeedVR2 video upscaling acts as a video upscaler so you can raise footage resolution in the same place you handle stills.

A few honest limits are worth setting up front. Most of these models do their best work at 2x to 4x. Higher factors like 8x amplify artifacts because the model has to invent more detail from a thin source, so a 4k upscale from a sharp medium photo beats a giant jump from a tiny one. Do not over-upscale a low-signal image and expect miracles. Text is the other trap: when you upscale a sign, a label, or a logo, the model will confidently produce letters that look crisp but are often wrong. There is no clean fix for that today, so treat any text in an upscaled frame as suspect and check it by eye.

Because every step is open, you can act as a real image enhancer rather than work with a black box. You set the model, the denoise level, and how aggressively to add detail, then re-run any pass instead of accepting one fixed output. There is no watermark on the download. If your goal is to enlarge photo without losing quality, start conservative, compare against the original at full zoom, and push the detail only as far as the texture stays believable. That workflow, run on Comfy Cloud or locally in ComfyUI, is how you increase image resolution without trading away the look of a real photograph.

styles.description: Use SeedVR2 to upscale image content cleanly while keeping pores, edges, and natural texture intact.

styles.description: Pick Magnific creative to add invented detail for a richer look, or Magnific precise to stay faithful to the source.

styles.title: Video upscaling
styles.description: Raise footage resolution with the SeedVR2 video upscaler so stills and clips live in one place.

howToUse: Choose the model for your job: SeedVR2 for general work, Z-Image-Turbo for a fast 2K pass, Magnific for creative or precise detail, Topaz or Stability for photo enhancement.
Open the workflow on Comfy Cloud with no install, or load it in your local ComfyUI.
Upload the image or clip you want to enlarge, using the sharpest source you have.
Set the upscale factor to 2x or 4x and start the denoise and detail strength conservative.
Run the pass, then compare the result against the original at full zoom to check skin and texture.
Lower the detail strength and re-run if faces look plastic, then download the version you like at full resolution.
capabilitiesIntro: Enlarge stills and footage while keeping real skin and texture, choosing the model and detail strength for each job.

faqItems.question: How does an ai image upscaler avoid making faces look plastic?
faqItems.answer: Plastic skin happens when an upscaler replaces lost micro-texture, like pores and fine wrinkles, with smoothing. These workflows let you pick a texture-preserving model such as SeedVR2 and lower the denoise and detail strength yourself. If a pass looks waxy, you dial it back and re-run instead of accepting one fixed output.

faqItems.question: What is the difference between AI upscaling and a normal resize?
faqItems.answer: A normal resize stretches the pixels you already have, so the photo gets bigger and softer with no new information. AI upscaling reconstructs detail instead, predicting the edges and surfaces a higher-resolution version would contain. That reconstruction is why it can sharpen a face, and also why the wrong model can over-smooth skin.

faqItems.question: How far can I upscale an image before it breaks down?
faqItems.answer: Most of these models do their best work between 2x and 4x. Pushing to 8x or beyond amplifies artifacts because the model has to invent more detail from a thin source. A 4k upscale from a sharp medium-sized photo will look far better than a huge jump from a tiny, low-signal image.

faqItems.question: Can I upscale text and expect it to stay accurate?
faqItems.answer: No, and it is important to set this expectation honestly. When you enlarge a sign, label, or logo, the model often produces letters that look crisp but are wrong. There is no clean fix for text hallucination today, so check any text in an upscaled frame by eye and do not rely on it.

faqItems.question: Is there a reliable way to enlarge photo without losing quality?
faqItems.answer: Start from the sharpest source you have and keep the factor at 2x or 4x. Choose a model that fits the job, then start the detail strength low and raise it only as far as the texture stays believable. Comparing against the original at full zoom after each pass is the most reliable way to keep the result faithful.

faqItems.question: Can I run these upscaling workflows myself, and is there a watermark?
faqItems.answer: The workflows are open and editable. You can run them locally in ComfyUI, or on Comfy Cloud with no setup. There is no watermark on the download, so you can iterate and re-export as many versions as you want.

faqItems.question: Does this work as a video upscaler too, or only for stills?
faqItems.answer: Both. SeedVR2 video upscaling raises footage resolution, so you can handle clips in the same place you upscale stills. The same honest limits apply: keep the factor modest and start from clean source footage for the best result.

faqItems.question: Why does a higher upscale factor sometimes look worse?
faqItems.answer: A bigger factor forces the model to invent more detail from the same limited source, which amplifies any noise or compression already in the image. The invented detail starts to drift from what the real surface looked like. Staying at 2x to 4x keeps the reconstruction grounded in actual information from your photo.

faqItems.question: Do I need to install anything to run these workflows?
faqItems.answer: No. Comfy Cloud opens the same workflows in your browser on remote GPUs, so there is nothing to download or set up. If you prefer to keep everything on your own machine, the identical graphs also load in a local ComfyUI install.

faqItems.question: What VRAM do I need to run these upscalers locally?
faqItems.answer: It depends on the model. SeedVR2 runs comfortably on a 24GB card such as an RTX 4090, and the 7B variant fits in roughly 16GB with block swap, while GGUF-quantized builds with tiled VAE drop the floor to about 8 to 12GB. Comfy Cloud is the alternative when your card is too small for the resolution you want.

faqItems.question: Does running this cost anything, and how is it priced?
faqItems.answer: Comfy Cloud is a paid plan: it uses a credit pool spent on active workflow runtime, with tiered seats for heavier use. Running the same workflows locally in ComfyUI uses your own GPU with no per-image charge. Either way the model weights for options like SeedVR2 are open.
